organizations.name: AbCellera Biologics
organizations.shortBlurb: AbCellera Biologics, a Vancouver-based public company specializing in AI-driven antibody therapeutics, has 596 employees and reported a revenue of $28.83M, down 24% year-over-year. Founded in 2012, it raised $483M in its IPO in December 2020 and is known for its AI-powered antibody search platform and partnerships with global pharma. Key contact is CEO Carl Hansen.

organizations.name: Zymeworks
organizations.shortBlurb: Zymeworks is a public company based in Vancouver, focusing on AI in data science and healthcare, with over 300 employees. The company reported $140M in revenue for 2023 and has a market cap of $650M as of July 2025. Notable projects include AI-enhanced drug discovery platforms.

organizations.name: Finn AI
organizations.keyPeople: Jake Tyler (CEO), Natalie Cartwright (COO), Guru Atlu (CTO) - AI-powered virtual assistant for banks (Source: Company data)
organizations.shortBlurb: Finn AI is a Vancouver-based scale-up specializing in AI-powered virtual assistants for banks, with over $30M in funding and notable clients like BMO and Visa. Founded in 2014, it was acquired by Glia in 2023, contributing to Glia's unicorn status. The company has a team of 60 and offers solutions for over 500 banking queries.

organizations.name: MetaOptima (DermEngine)
organizations.aiFocusAreas: Healthcare AI, Computer Vision, Data Science
organizations.shortBlurb: MetaOptima, a Vancouver-based scale-up founded in 2012, focuses on AI in healthcare, particularly through its dermatology platform. The company has raised over $8M in funding and employs around 30 people, with an estimated annual revenue of $5-10M for 2024. Key personnel include CEO Maryam Sadeghi and CTO Majid Razmara. Contact information includes a phone number and links to their website and LinkedIn profile.

organizations.name: Boast AI
organizations.funding: $23M Series A (March 2022) - Radian Capital, Craft Ventures, BDC Capital, Pender Ventures
organizations.shortBlurb: Boast AI, founded in 2017, is an AI company with 100-200 employees and an estimated revenue of $10-15M ARR. It has raised $23M in Series A funding from investors including Radian Capital and Craft Ventures. Key people include CEO and Co-founder Alex Popa.

organizations.name: GreenMeter AI
organizations.funding: Government grants - Government programs, SDTC, NRC IRAP
organizations.shortBlurb: GreenMeter AI is an AI company based in Vancouver, BC, founded in 2021, with an employee count of 10-25. It has received government grants for funding and is currently generating early revenue.

organizations.name: Radical Entertainment
organizations.shortBlurb: Radical Entertainment is a game development studio located in Vancouver, BC, founded in 2018 as an Activision subsidiary. It was previously shut down in 2012 and reopened in 2023. Notable projects include the Prototype series and Tony Hawk games.

organizations.name: Roomvu
organizations.funding: Backed by NAR REACH (National Association of Realtors)
organizations.aiFocusAreas: Real estate AI, Content generation, Marketing automation, PropTech
organizations.shortBlurb: AI-powered real estate marketing platform generating hyperlocal video content. Serves 95,000+ agents. CIX 2024 Top 20 Early Award winner. Expanded to US in 2024.

organizations.name: Phaidra
organizations.funding:
$12M Series A (July 2024)

Led by Index Ventures
Total raised: $60.5M
organizations.aiFocusAreas: Industrial AI, Energy optimization, Data center cooling, Reinforcement learning
organizations.keyPeople:
Jim Gao (CEO & Co-founder, ex-DeepMind Energy lead)
Vedavyas Panneershelvam (Co-founder, ex-DeepMind)
Katherine Hoffman (Co-founder)
organizations.shortBlurb: AI virtual plant operator for industrial automation. Founded by ex-DeepMind team. Reduces data center energy consumption by up to 30%. $12M Series A in 2024.

organizations.name: AgriSense AI
organizations.shortBlurb: AgriSense AI, founded in 2025, is a pre-seed stage company based in Kelowna, BC, focusing on AI applications in agriculture, particularly crop monitoring and yield prediction. The team consists of 3-7 members and is involved in early pilot revenue projects within the AgTech ecosystem.

organizations.name: Forest AI Systems
organizations.shortBlurb: Forest AI Systems, founded in 2025, focuses on environmental monitoring and forestry AI, with a notable project in AI forest health monitoring. The company is in the pre-seed funding stage and is based in Prince George, BC, with a small team of 2-6 employees.

organizations.name: Voltaiq Energy AI
organizations.aiFocusAreas: Energy AI, Battery Technology, Predictive Analytics
organizations.shortBlurb: Voltaiq Energy AI is a seed-stage company founded in 2025, focusing on battery technology and energy AI, with notable projects in battery AI optimization. The team consists of 3-8 employees, including former members from Tesla and Ballard Power, and is based in Burnaby, BC. The company is currently pre-revenue and relies on manual research for data sourcing.

organizations.name: Riverlane Solutions
organizations.funding: $4M Seed (2023) - Panache Ventures, N49P, Ascend Ventures
organizations.shortBlurb: Riverlane Solutions, founded in 2021, has an employee count of 10-25 and has raised $4M in seed funding from Panache Ventures, N49P, and Ascend Ventures. Key person includes CEO and co-founder Robert Howe, with early revenue reported.

organizations.name: Motive.io
organizations.shortBlurb: No content

organizations.name: SkyHive
organizations.shortBlurb: Reskill the world by organizing global workforce data
organizations.cityRegion: 815 Hastings St Fl 8, Vancouver, BC V6C 3N9

organizations.name: VRIFY Technology
organizations.keyPeople: Steve de Jong (Co-Founder & CEO) - Former CEO Integra Gold, grew company from $10M to $590M sale to Eldorado Gold, YMP Mining Professional 2017; JP Paiement (VP AI), Richard Cyphus (COO), Robbie McFarland (CFO), Michael Preuss (VP Marketing)
organizations.shortBlurb: Uses advanced AI to build DORA, an AI-assisted mineral discovery platform that analyses geological datasets to locate mineralization

organizations.name: Digital Technology Supercluster Healthtech Initiative
organizations.funding: {"totalRaised":"$450M+","rounds":[{"type":"Government Investment","amount":"$153M","date":"2019","leadInvestor":"Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada","otherInvestors":[],"description":"Federal government investment through Innovation Superclusters Initiative"},{"type":"Industry Co-Investment","amount":"$300M+","date":"2019-2025","leadInvestor":"Industry Partners","otherInvestors":["Microsoft","TELUS","Amazon","SAP","Teck Resources"],"description":"Matched industry investment leveraging government funding"}]}
organizations.shortBlurb: The initiative focuses on healthcare AI in Vancouver's Lower Mainland, with a goal to grow from 15 to over 50 employees by 2025 and engage over 450 member organizations. It has raised over $450M, including $153M from the government and $300M+ from industry partners like Microsoft and Amazon. The initiative has generated over $1.5B in economic activity, launched over 100 collaborative projects, and created more than 5,000 jobs, with notable projects in digital health solutions and precision health tools.

organizations.name: Clio
organizations.funding: $386M USD Total (2021-04) - Series E led by NEA, T. Rowe Price (Source: TechCrunch)
organizations.shortBlurb: Clio, a legal tech leader based in Vancouver, BC, has over 1,000 employees and a valuation of $3 billion as of 2024. The company raised $386 million in Series E funding and reported an annual revenue of over $150 million. Founded in 2008, notable projects include a data validation update and a Series F funding round in June 2024.

organizations.name: Artificial Intelligence Underwriting Company (AIUC)
organizations.shortBlurb: Insurance for AI systems

organizations.name: FreshGrade
organizations.funding: $11.7M Series A (2014) - NewSchools Venture Fund, Relay Ventures, Rethink Education
organizations.shortBlurb: FreshGrade is a startup founded in 2011, located in Kelowna, with an employee count of 50-100 and annual revenue exceeding $5M. It has raised $11.7M in Series A funding from investors including NewSchools Venture Fund and Relay Ventures. Key person associated is Lane Merrifield, the former CEO.
